title: catha transvaalensis
intro: Catha transvaalensis, also known as the South African catha or African khat, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to southern Africa. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in folk medicine for their stimulating and medicinal properties, which include acting as a natural stimulant and potential treatment for fatigue, anxiety, and depression. The health benefits of Catha transvaalensis include its potential to improve cognitive function and boost energy levels, as well as its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be consumed fresh or dried, and are often brewed into a tea or infused in hot water to release their medicinal compounds.
